title: carya glabra var. glabra
intro: Carya glabra var. glabra, also known as the smoothbark hickory or smoothbark shagbark hickory, is a deciduous tree native to North America, belonging to the family Juglandaceae. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The medicinal properties of Carya glabra var. glabra are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and promoting cardiovascular health. The bark can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal medicine.
